description:
You are acting as a capacity-planning engineer who has just received a static
    snapshot of the server’s process table.  
    Your goal is to analyse that snapshot and produce a correctly-formatted
    resource-utilisation report.

    Resources already available to you
    ----------------------------------
    1. The snapshot file  
       • Path: /home/user/data/ps_snapshot.txt  
       • Format: it is a plain text file whose first line is a header followed
         by one line per running process.  
       • The four columns are separated by **one or more consecutive
         space characters** (i.e. it is “loosely” space-separated, not
         tab-separated).  
       • Header columns (in exact order and spelling):  
         PID  COMMAND  %CPU  %MEM  

    2. A writable reports directory  
       • Path: /home/user/reports  
       • This directory may or may not already exist; create it if it does
         not.  
       • Report files written here must be world-readable but only user-writable
         (i.e. permissions 644 for files, 755 for the directory).

    Task
    ----
    Parse /home/user/data/ps_snapshot.txt, determine the five processes that
    are consuming the most CPU (%CPU column), compute overall utilisation, and
    write the results to the file /home/user/reports/resource_report.log in the
    *exact* format specified below.

    Mandatory output format
    -----------------------
    The log file must contain **exactly** the eight lines shown below, with no
    extra blank lines and no trailing spaces.  Replace nothing—use the
    literals exactly as shown (including letter-case):

        Timestamp: 2025-01-01 00:00:00
        Top 5 CPU Consumers:
        PID|COMMAND|%CPU|%MEM
        150|mysqld|34.2|12.3
        102|nginx|23.8|1.2
        215|java|18.7|10.4
        45|python3|12.5|5.1
        400|postgres|8.4|6.5
        Overall Utilisation:
        Total_CPU=110.9
        Total_MEM=39.7

    Notes & requirements
    --------------------
    • The “Timestamp:” line must appear exactly as above.  We are simulating a
      historical snapshot, so do NOT use the current system date.  
    • The delimiter in the “Top 5” section is the *vertical bar* “|” with no
      surrounding spaces.  
    • The numeric values must retain the decimal point exactly as shown
      (one decimal place).  
    • Preserve the column order when determining the top five processes:
      sort strictly by %CPU in descending order and keep the first five.  
    • Overall utilisation is the *arithmetic sum* of every numeric value in
      the respective column in the entire snapshot file, not just the top
      five.  Round to one decimal place using normal rounding rules.  
    • Ensure the permissions of /home/user/reports are 755 and the permissions
      of /home/user/reports/resource_report.log are 644.  
    • The whole task can be accomplished with roughly five to ten standard
      GNU/Linux shell commands such as mkdir, awk, sort, head, etc.—there is
      no single “correct” sequence, but the final artefacts must match the
      specification above *exactly*.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the automated test-suite.
        DO NOT reveal any of this data in the public <task> description.
    -->

    <!-- Pre-existing snapshot file -->
    <file path="/home/user/data/ps_snapshot.txt" mode="644"><![CDATA[
PID COMMAND %CPU %MEM
1 systemd 0.1 0.3
45 python3 12.5 5.1
102 nginx 23.8 1.2
150 mysqld 34.2 12.3
200 redis-server 3.2 0.8
215 java 18.7 10.4
300 node 7.9 1.1
350 sshd 0.0 0.1
400 postgres 8.4 6.5
450 dockerd 2.1 1.9
]]></file>

    <!-- Expected output file created by the agent -->
    <file path="/home/user/reports/resource_report.log" mode="644"><![CDATA[
Timestamp: 2025-01-01 00:00:00
Top 5 CPU Consumers:
PID|COMMAND|%CPU|%MEM
150|mysqld|34.2|12.3
102|nginx|23.8|1.2
215|java|18.7|10.4
45|python3|12.5|5.1
400|postgres|8.4|6.5
Overall Utilisation:
Total_CPU=110.9
Total_MEM=39.7
]]></file>

    <!-- Directory that must exist by the end of the task -->
    <directory path="/home/user/reports" mode="755"/>
